channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Chicago prepares for deportation arrests targeting hundreds next week after Trump takes office
item.description: Federal immigration officers will target more than 300 people with histories of egregious, violent crimes after President-elect Donald Trump takes office Monday, an official said, marking his administration's initial attempt toward fulfilling his promise of large-scale deportations.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Thousands march in Washington days before Trump takes office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of people from around the United States were rallying in the nation's capital Saturday for women's reproductive rights and other causes they believe are under threat from the incoming Trump administration, reprising the original Women's March days before President-elect D
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, ASHRAF KHALIL and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump is arriving in Washington for inaugural celebrations to mark his return to power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump will return to Washington Saturday to kick off days of pageantry heralding his second inauguration as president, four years after he departed the city under the shadow of an attack by his supporters on the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Journalists in Haiti demand justice as they bury a second colleague killed by gangs
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — The young boy wore a suit and bow tie to his stepfather’s funeral on Saturday, where he demanded a handkerchief and wiped tears off his mother’s face as they buried Marckendy Natoux, one of two journalists killed by gangs in Haiti.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judges allow some Capitol riot defendants to return to DC for Trump's inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of Donald Trump supporters stormed the U.S. Capitol after he lost the 2020 presidential election. Four years later, some of them are allowed to return to the nation's capital so they can celebrate Trump's return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Extreme cold forecast for much of US in coming days
item.description: Another blast of winter weather is expected in parts of the U.S. in the coming days, including bone-chilling wind in the Northern Plains and unusual snow and ice in the Gulf Coast area.
item.dc:creator: Jack Dura And Julie Walker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How to build a budget to help you save in 2025
item.description: NewsNation spoke with a financial expert about how to build a budget that could help you start the year off with less financial stress.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Who is Sean Curran? Head of Trump's personal detail to be nominated for Secret Service director
item.description: Sean Curran, the head of President-elect Trump's personal security detail, will be tapped to lead the U.S. Secret Service, according to the president's son.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Navajo Nation firefighters battle wildfires in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters from the Navajo Nation worked tirelessly through a haze of dust to cut away dirt from a narrow road at the side of a mountain struck by a landslide in Southern California, coughing and sneezing amid the backbreaking work.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LOCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justices agree to hear Maryland case on parents' rights and LGBTQ books
item.description: The parents, represented by the Becket Fund for Religious Liberty, sued Montgomery County Public Schools in 2023 after the school district said it would no longer honor opt-out requests or notify families if a book referencing gender or sexuality would be read in class.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: On LA fire lines, inmates shoulder heavy packs and tackle dangerous work for less than $30 a day
item.description: Over 1,100 California inmates have worked around the clock to help fight Los Angeles-area fires that have killed at least 25 people and destroyed thousands of homes.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber And Dorany Pineda, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Teacher charged with allegedly sexually assaulting former student, having his child
item.description: A New Jersey teacher is facing charges after police say she is accused of sexually abusing one of her students and having his child.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Cristelli, KTLA 5

item.title: Senate advances Laken Riley Act, teeing up final vote
item.description: The Senate voted Friday to advance the Laken Riley Act, putting Republicans a step closer to sending the first bill of the 119th Congress to President-elect Trump for his signature next week and giving him an early win on one of his key issues.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Bruce Willis shakes hands with first responders in rare public appearance
item.description: Amid the devastating wildfires in Southern California, Bruce Willis took time to thank first responders.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House says TikTok ban will fall to Trump administration
item.description: The White House said Friday that the TikTok ban will fall to President-elect Trump's administration after the Supreme Court upheld a law requiring the app's China-based parent company to divest from it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden got an Oval Office letter from Trump and may leave one in the desk himself
item.description: Ronald Reagan probably didn't realize he was starting a tradition when he wrote a note congratulating his successor and left it in the Oval Office desk drawer after two terms as president.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FDA will allow Zyn nicotine pouches to stay on the market
item.description: Zyn products will be allowed to stay on the market after federal health officials said Thursday that the popular nicotine pouches can help adult smokers cut back or switch completely.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court upholds TikTok ban
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday unanimously upheld a law requiring TikTok’s China-based parent company to divest from the app, teeing up a ban set to take effect Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court gives green light to law that could ban TikTok
item.description: WASHINGTON — The Supreme Court on Friday delivered a blow to TikTok by upholding a law that could potentially lead to the video-sharing social media platform being banned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley and Kat Tenbarge, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Capital One technical issue delays payments for users: What to know
item.description: Some Capital One users were still waiting to receive deposits and payments on Friday morning after the company experienced a processing disruption.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WGN 9

item.title: Mel Gibson ‘just as surprised’ as anyone to be named Trump ‘special envoy’ to Hollywood
item.description: Actor and film producer Mel Gibson said he was stunned to be appointed a special envoy to Hollywood under the upcoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Pepsi discontinues 'permanent' flavor, debuts a completely different one
item.description: Pepsi is planning to debut a brand-new flavor this month, marking its first “permanent” addition to the brand’s beverage portfolio in four years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Noem pledges to eliminate CBP One app; Senate advances Laken Riley Act
item.description: South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem (R), President-elect Trump's pick to head the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), is appearing for her confirmation hearing before a Senate panel Friday. The purview of DHS involves immigration and federal emergency management — two hot-button topics.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, WGN 9

item.title: Biden got an Oval Office letter from Trump and may leave one in the desk himself. It’d be a first
item.description: Every president since Ronald Reagan has left a note for his successor, and President Joe Biden could be the first to write a letter to someone who is both his successor and the predecessor who left a note for him.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Southern California native paid off mortgage day before Eaton Fire consumed home
item.description: While those displaced by the Eaton and Palisades fires are still reeling from the loss of their homes and, in some cases, loved ones, many of them say they’re being victimized yet again by price-gouging landlords.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: SpaceX loses spacecraft after catching rocket booster at the launch pad in latest Starship test
item.description: SpaceX launched its Starship rocket on its latest test flight Thursday, but the spacecraft was destroyed following a thrilling booster catch back at the pad. Elon Musk’s company said Starship broke apart — what it called a “rapid unscheduled disassembly.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Giuliani settles legal fight with former Georgia election workers and agrees to stop defaming them
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rudy Giuliani reached a deal Thursday that lets the cash-strapped ex-New York City mayor keep his homes and belongings, including prized World Series rings, in exchange for unspecified compensation and a promise to never again speak ill of two former Georgia elections workers who won
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Where are they? Help sought finding those missing after Eaton Fire in California
item.description: Authorities are asking the public for help in their search for several Altadena residents who remain unaccounted for after the deadly Eaton Fire burned thousands of homes.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KRON4

item.title: PHOTOS: 'Home Alone' house in Illinois sells for $250K over asking price
item.description: A famous house featured in the iconic holiday film "Home Alone" sold this week for nearly $300,000 over its asking price.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KRON4

item.title: TikTok CEO attending Trump inauguration
item.description: TikTok CEO Shou Zi Chew will attend President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration next week, one day after a potential ban of the video-sharing platform could take effect in the U.S., according to a source familiar with the plans.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: United Airlines flight returns to O'Hare after hitting coyote during takeoff
item.description: A United Airlines flight from Chicago to Phoenix had to return to O'Hare International Airport on Sunday morning after its landing gear struck a coyote during takeoff.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, ABC 4

item.title: Man sought after 'So I raped you' Facebook message being extradited to US
item.description: French Appeal Court prosecutors said an American man accused of sexually assaulting a Pennsylvania college student in 2013 and later sending her a Facebook message that said, “So I raped you," was being extradited Thursday to the United States.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — More than a week into what could be the costliest wildfires in U.S. history, weary and anxious Los Angeles-area residents were hoping for a favorable shift in the winds expected Thursday and an end to the constant fear that their lives might be further upended.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The long struggle to establish Martin Luther King Jr. Day
item.description: The Rev. Martin Luther King Jr. gave his iconic “I Have a Dream” speech in 1963 on the steps of the Lincoln Memorial. He chose that location in part to honor President Abraham Lincoln as “a great American, in whose symbolic shadow we stand today.” Now, millions of people honor King in the same way.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles fire captain loses home to Eaton Fire
item.description: A man tasked with protecting the Los Angeles area from raging wildfires now ranks among those who have lost their homes and everything inside.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, Fox 5

item.title: Officials tout Gaza ceasefire, hostage deal as Israel says details still in flux
item.description: Israel and Hamas agreed to pause the devastating war in the Gaza Strip, mediators announced Wednesday, raising the possibility of winding down the deadliest and most destructive fighting between the bitter enemies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
